notes: Ninth validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its mission says the school equips students from all backgrounds to succeed in the college and career of their choice through academic excellence, strong character, and an entrepreneurial mindset.
Official branding describes it as a tuition-free charter school established in 2014.
The school's mission uniquely emphasizes an entrepreneurial mindset alongside academics and character.
The board page says the governing board consists of five community members who oversee policy, state compliance, budget allocations, and educational goals.
Its news section features students called the "Young Moguls" leading a community giving effort.
Official board pages list Dr. Terry Cline as Board Chair and Laila Minott as Executive Director.
Its mission says the school equips students with the tools of learning to think critically, reason effectively, and communicate persuasively through the rigors of a classical education.
The school says its distinct virtues are captured in the acronym R.A.I.S.E.: Respect, Accountability, Integrity, Service, and Excellence.
Official materials explain its classical education philosophy through the three stages of learning: grammar, logic, and rhetoric.
Its curriculum highlights Latin instruction alongside core subjects to deepen language structure and vocabulary.
The board page says regular board meetings are held on the third Tuesday of each month at 4:45 p.m.
Official pages list Pamela Franklin as Director and Rev. Martin McCarthy as board chair.

Its mission says the school, in partnership with parents and community members, fosters student learning and achievement and inspires students to become independent thinkers, effective communicators, and contributing citizens of a global society.
Its "DCA Difference" highlights community, excellence, achievement, empathy, independence, and communication.
The homepage says the school opened in 2017.
Official materials say the school continues to add programs and academic clubs that reflect the diversity of students' skills and interests.
The board page lists Amanda Whitaker as Chairperson.
The staff directory lists Joseph Wood as C.E.O. and Dr. Tina Alexander as Principal.
Its mission says the school offers a classical, knowledge-based education that fosters critical thinking, civic responsibilities, and virtue for all.
Official materials say the school uses an engaging Core Knowledge curriculum aligned to the North Carolina Standard Course of Study.
The school highlights Outdoor Exploration, Classical Education, and Lifelong Learning as key themes on its homepage.
Its about page says students can earn certificates in fields such as computer science, welding, and machining through a partnership with Isothermal Community College.
The board page says the board usually meets on the third Thursday of the month at 3:30 p.m.
Official board materials list Randy Nelson as Chair of the Board of Directors.

Its mission says the school facilitates the development of college-ready individuals through rigorous academics and expectations of honesty, respect, empathy, responsibility, service, and preparedness.
Its vision describes the school as the premier college prep community for highly engaged educators and learners.
Official history says the school obtained its charter in 1998.
The school says students document service hours every year through its Learn. Serve. Engage. program.
Its priorities include dual college enrollment and required volunteer hours for parents.
The overview page includes a message from Chief Administrator Jonathan Bryant.
